name: Genesis/JSONFlow
description: System-compliant Genesis event-sourced module spec for the JSONFlow compilation pipeline. Defines the canonical 3-stage pipeline: NL → Genesis Module → JSONFlow IR → Multi-Language Code.

stage_1.label: Genesis Module
stage_1.description: Natural language input → deterministic Genesis JSON module via LLM (Ollama/Claude). Enforces all 11 canonical invariants.

output.description: Genesis module JSON conforming to canonical schema

stage_2.label: JSONFlow IR
stage_2.description: Genesis module → JSONFlow Intermediate Representation (deterministic compiler, no LLM). Produces deriveState, scheduleNextAction, computeSignals, emitEvents programs.

stage_3.label: Code Output
stage_3.description: JSONFlow IR → target language code (Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Rust, Go, Java, C#, Swift, Kotlin).

derived_state.description: Full deterministic state derived by replaying the event log. Tracks all module entities, counters, and signal values.

emitted_events.description: Canonical sequence of events emitted by the Genesis module reducers. Order is deterministic given identical event log.

ModuleCreated.description: Emitted when a new Genesis module is successfully compiled from natural language.

JSONFlowCompiled.description: Emitted when a Genesis module is compiled to JSONFlow IR programs.

CodeCompiled.description: Emitted when a JSONFlow program is compiled to target language code.

ValidationFailed.description: Emitted when a Genesis module fails canonical deterministic validation.

deriveState.description: Replay eventLog to build deterministic pipeline state. Tracks module count, compilation stages reached, and language usage.

energy.description: Rate of ModuleCreated events in the last 100 events relative to total.

decay.description: Staleness of the pipeline — how long since the last compilation event.

priority.description: Combined pipeline priority signal weighted by energy and freshness.

scheduleNextAction.description: Select next pipeline action based on current stage and signal values. Returns one of: RunGenesis | CompileJSONFlow | CompileCode | Idle.

invariants: No hidden or mutable state outside the log
All outputs traceable to explicit events
Signals are functions of history, not stored values
Same input sequence → identical execution path
Same event log → identical emitted event sequence
Every event has a deterministic total ordering key
Reducers are pure functions of (state, event)
Schedulers are pure functions of event history with deterministic tie-breaking
Signal functions are pure functions of event history
Given the same event log, emitted event sequence is identical

ide_config.description: Configuration for the JSONFlow Genesis IDE (Remix-style interface)
